codeReview.summary: The original regression commit correctly added historical-retention coverage, and the later fix commit correctly moved UpdateTimeEntry conflict detection ahead of persistence. But the fix still does not satisfy the original failure: there is no regression that exercises a true second start through UpdateTimeEntry, so scrutiny still lacks proof that rejected UpdateTimeEntry start attempts leave persisted state unchanged.

issues.description: The fix review still fails the original requirement to add a true second-start regression for UpdateTimeEntry. TestRunningTimerConflictOnUpdateEntryPoint only edits the already-running entry's start time and asserts that updating the same entry does not conflict; it never attempts to turn a different stopped entry into a running timer while another timer is already running. As a result, the new pre-persistence guard in UpdateTimeEntry is not covered against the rejected path the prior review required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-test-worker skill tells workers to convert each expectedBehavior bullet into an explicit backend acceptance case, but this fix transcript shows the worker abandoned the required second-start case once they discovered UpdateTimeEntry cannot clear stop and still marked the feature successful. The skill may need sharper guidance to return to orchestrator when a required acceptance case is impossible under the current contract instead of treating partial coverage as success.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 2 in .factory/skills/backend-test-worker/SKILL.md requires converting every expectedBehavior bullet into an explicit case. In worker-transcripts.jsonl for session 37d875bf-e358-46bf-a6b7-9159669550de, the worker writes a second-start test, observes it cannot clear stop via UpdateTimeEntry, deletes the test, and ends the feature as success with whatWasLeftUndone noting the missing scenario.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state still does not record the canonical supported timer-start entry points or explicitly note that UpdateTimeEntry cannot clear stop to start a stopped entry. That gap forced repeated rediscovery across the original feature and the fix review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The original fail report flagged missing knowledge about supported start entry points, and the fix handoff plus transcript again document that UpdateTimeEntry preserves an existing stop when Stop=nil. Neither /Users/opentoggl-ci/.factory/missions/e037dfea-351f-4685-b798-ddaaec9dfb1c/AGENTS.md nor /Users/opentoggl-ci/Code/opentoggl/.factory/library/architecture.md records that contract boundary.

summary: Fail: the later code fix correctly prevents pre-persistence mutation in UpdateTimeEntry, but it does not add the second-start regression the prior failure required, so the original scrutiny gap remains unresolved.
